title: Tanarius peltatus

intro: Tanarius peltatus, also known as Bugle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and relieving respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tanarius peltatus include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
